enonce.texte: Match the words on the left with their correct definitions on the right. This exercise will test your knowledge of key vocabulary related to job applications and interviews.

questions.texte: Match each term to its definition.

sousQuestions.reponse: d. A formal meeting where a candidate is asked questions to determine their suitability for a job.
e. A written document summarizing a person's education, work experience, and skills.
a. Official degrees, diplomas, or certificates that show you are competent for a job.
b. The knowledge or skill acquired by doing a job over a period of time.
c. The ability to do an activity or job well, especially because you have practiced it.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences below using the correct tense: the Present Perfect (to talk about experiences) or the Past Simple (to talk about a specific time in the past).

questions.texte: Choose the correct verb form for each sentence.
questions.reponse: 1. have applied, 2. worked, 3. Have you ever had, 4. studied, 5. has never used

sousQuestions.texte: I (apply) _______ for three different jobs this month.
She (work) _______ as a cashier at the supermarket two years ago.
He (study) _______ law at the University of Yaoundé from 2018 to 2022.
My brother (never / use) _______ a computer for work.

enonce.texte:
Situation: You are applying for the position of Sales Assistant at a new bookstore in Douala called "Readers' Haven." Below is the job advertisement. Your friend will play the role of the store manager, Mr. Nguema.

Task: With a partner, simulate a 3-4 minute job interview. First, introduce yourself. Then, answer the manager's questions to discuss your qualifications and experience. Finally, ask two polite questions about the job using the modals can, could, or may.
images: A simple, realistic job advertisement poster for a bookstore called 'Readers' Haven' in Douala. The ad is for a 'Sales Assistant' position. It lists requirements: 'Good communication skills, ability to work on weekends, basic math skills.' It also includes a contact: 'Apply in person to Mr. Nguema, Store Manager.' The design is clean and professional, with a book icon.

questions.texte: Prepare answers for the following questions that Mr. Nguema might ask you. Write your answers in complete sentences.
questions.reponse: Answers will vary but should be grammatically correct and relevant.

sousQuestions.texte: Introduce yourself and tell me about your educational background.
sousQuestions.reponse: (Example) 'Good morning, Mr. Nguema. My name is Marie Ndongo. I am a Terminale student at Lycée de Bonabéri, and I am studying literature and English.'

sousQuestions.texte: Why do you want to work at 'Readers' Haven'?
sousQuestions.reponse: (Example) 'I am passionate about reading, and I believe I can help customers find books they will love. This job would give me valuable experience in customer service.'

sousQuestions.texte: What are your main strengths or skills for this position?
sousQuestions.reponse: (Example) 'I have good communication skills from participating in school debates. I am also organized and can handle cash transactions accurately.'

questions.texte: Write two polite questions you would ask Mr. Nguema at the end of the interview. Use 'can', 'could', or 'may'.
questions.reponse: Answers will vary but must use polite modals.

sousQuestions.reponse: (Example) 'Could you tell me what the typical work schedule is for a Sales Assistant?'
(Example) 'May I ask if there is a training period for new employees?'

intitule: Analyzing a Job Interview: Strengths and Weaknesses

enonce.texte: Read the transcript of a short excerpt from a job interview for a Junior Web Developer position. The candidate is Paul. Then, answer the questions that follow.

sousQuestions.texte:
Interviewer: 'So, Paul, tell me about your experience with web development.'
Paul: 'Well, I have learned HTML and CSS. I am a fast learner. I am very good at video games.'
Interviewer: 'I see. And have you ever worked on a team project before?'
Paul: 'Yes, I have worked on one. It was okay. I prefer to work alone because it is faster.'
Interviewer: 'Where do you see yourself in five years?'
Paul: 'I don't know. Maybe with a job. I need money.'

questions.texte: Identify two weaknesses in Paul's answers. Use specific examples from the text to justify your analysis.
questions.reponse: Weakness 1: He mentions video games, which is not relevant to web development. Weakness 2: He says he prefers to work alone, showing a lack of teamwork skills, which is important for most jobs. Weakness 3: His answer about the future is vague and focuses on money ('I need money'), which seems unprofessional.

questions.texte: Rewrite Paul's answer to the last question ('Where do you see yourself in five years?') to make it more professional and effective.
questions.reponse: (Example) 'In five years, I see myself as a skilled full-stack developer, leading small projects and contributing to innovative web solutions for this company. I am committed to growing my skills and taking on more responsibility.'

questions.texte: Evaluate the importance of the phrase 'I am a fast learner' in a job interview. Explain why this statement, without evidence, might not be convincing to an employer.
questions.reponse: The phrase 'I am a fast learner' is a common cliché. An employer might find it unconvincing without evidence because anyone can say it. To be effective, Paul should provide a concrete example, such as 'I am a fast learner; for example, I taught myself the basics of JavaScript in two weeks using online tutorials.' This shows proof of the claim.
